questions.question: The diagram shows a root hair cell from a lemon tree and some soil particles.

parts.question: State one feature, visible in the diagram, which shows that a root hair cell is a plant cell.
parts.answer: cell wall or large / central / sap vacuole

parts.question: Describe how mineral ions pass from the soil into the root hair cell.
parts.answer: dissolved in water; through the cell wall; carriers in membrane; through the cell / partially permeable membrane; by active transport / diffusion; correct reference to concentration gradient; using energy (for active transport); from (aerobic) respiration (for active transport)

parts.question: The lemon tree has grown in the same soil for many years so the soil now lacks magnesium. Describe and explain the effect on the lemon tree of a lack of magnesium.
parts.answer: yellowing / chlorosis / reduced growth / cannot photosynthesise / make glucose / starch / sugar / carbohydrate; deficiency of chlorophyll

questions.question: A person visits an eye doctor to have an eye test. On the wall of the doctor's room is a diagram of an eye.

parts.question: Name the part labelled X.
parts.answer: lens

parts.question: Describe one way in which the structure of X is related to its function.
parts.answer: transparent / spherical / curved sides; allows light to pass through / to focus / refract light; or flexible / elastic / can change shape; accommodation / focus objects

parts.question: Name layer Y.
parts.answer: retina

parts.question: Name structure Z.
parts.answer: macula / yellow spot / fovea

parts.question: Layer Y does not contain blood vessels. Explain how the blood vessels in the choroid layer are important for layer Y.
parts.answer: bring glucose / amino acids / minerals / vitamins; for energy / repair of cells; bring oxygen; for (aerobic) respiration; take urea / carbon dioxide away; diffusion; capillaries / veins / arteries mentioned in correct context; bring white blood cells; to fight infection

parts.question: When the bright light is shone into the eye there is a pupil reflex which causes the pupil to become smaller. Explain how named parts of the eye and of the nervous system are involved in this pupil reflex.
parts.answer: retina / rods detect extra light (intensity); impulse + to CNS / brain; via optic nerve / sensory neurone; back to eye / iris / effector / muscle + motor neurone; radial muscles relax; circular muscles contract; iris becomes bigger / elongates; reflex arc

parts.question: Some people inherit a condition which prevents the production of the dark pigment melanin in their choroid and skin cells. The gene involved has two alleles, A (dominant) and a (recessive). Only people with two recessive alleles have this condition. Two people, each with a heterozygous genotype, have children together. Complete the genetic diagram to show the possible genotypes and phenotypes of the children.
parts.answer: parental genotypes = Aa + Aa; gametes a and A; offspring genotypes AA, Aa, Aa and aa; correctly linked phenotypes: AA & Aa = produce melanin, aa don't produce melanin

parts.question: State the probability of these two people having a child who does not produce melanin.

questions.question: A sycamore tree is a producer for many food chains in a forest ecosystem.

parts.question: Explain what is meant by a producer.
parts.answer: photosynthesis / autotrophic / making own organic nutrients

parts.question: Explain what is meant by a food chain.
parts.answer: flow / transfer + energy / food

parts.question: The diagrams show a pyramid of numbers and a pyramid of biomass for a sycamore tree. Explain the different shapes of the two pyramids.
parts.answer: biomass tree has large mass so long rectangle / correct reference to relative masses at two trophic levels; biomass declines as trophic level increases; numbers there is only one tree so a short rectangle for numbers / correct reference to relative numbers at two trophic levels; both or either after producer both pyramids in shape; energy is lost (to the environment); as heat / thermal energy; from respiration

parts.question: Sycamore trees produce flowers that are insect-pollinated. Suggest two features of flowers which adapt them for insect-pollination.
parts.answer: large / colourful + petals / sepals; nectaries / nectar; scent; anthers / stigma + inside petals; short / sticky stigma; markings / nectar guides; petals modified with insect landing site; pollen rough / sticky

parts.question: State the type of cell division that produces pollen grains.
parts.answer: meiosis

questions.question: The diagram shows part of the human alimentary canal and its associated organs.

parts.question: Complete the three labels on the diagram by naming the structures.
parts.answer: clockwise from top: oesophagus / gullet; duodenum / small intestine; gall bladder

parts.question: Describe the functions of the structure labelled F.
parts.answer: digestion / break down food or protein / protein to polypeptides / protein to amino acids; (mechanical) muscle / contractions / movement + make food particles smaller / chyme; (chemical) enzymes / proteases / pepsin + proteins; hydrochloric acid + optimum pH; hydrochloric acid + destroy microbes / defence against microbes; mucus + protects lining / lubrication

parts.question: Name structure H and describe its role in homeostasis.
parts.answer: pancreas; control of blood glucose concentration / level or produces / releases + insulin / glucagon

questions.question: A farmer is using a tool, called a mattock, to weed her crop.

parts.question: Explain how the muscles and bones in her right arm help her to lift the tool.
parts.answer: named bone(s) + location; antagonistic muscles; biceps contract; become shorter / radius or ulna is moved towards humerus; triceps relax; hinge joint

parts.question: The farmer has not used any insecticides or artificial fertilisers on the crop. Explain ways in which this may help the local environment.
parts.answer: no leaching / run-off into water / water pollution; so insecticides not ingested by aquatic insects; no bioaccumulation; (so fertilisers) don't cause algal bloom / eutrophication; eutrophication detail e.g. decomposition of algae / lack of oxygen / increase in bacteria; pollinators / insects that eat pests can still function; increase biodiversity; greater variety of food sources for consumers; if artificial fertilisers replaced with organic then soil structure maintained

parts.question: Outline the role of microorganisms in the process of yoghurt production.
parts.answer: heating / pasteurisation at start of process or after fermentation; denaturation of milk protein / kill unwanted bacteria; starter culture / bacteria; Lactobacillus (bulgaricus) / Streptococcus (thermophiles); fermentation / anaerobic respiration; warm temperature; lactose / milk sugar; lactic acid produced; decreases pH; coagulation / thickening

parts.question: Yoghurt can contribute to a balanced diet but adult humans cannot survive by eating only yoghurt. Explain this statement using the information on the label.
parts.answer: (yoghurt contributes to a balanced diet) carbohydrates for energy; proteins for growth / repair; water for chemical reactions / cytoplasm / transport; fats for energy / insulation; calcium for bones / prevent rickets; (yoghurt is not suitable on its own) no + fibre + so poor bowel health / constipation; no + iron; no + vitamin C or other named vitamin

questions.question: Excess amino acids are broken down in liver cells to form molecules of urea.

parts.question: Describe the pathway taken by a molecule of urea, from the liver of a mammal until it reaches the soil.
parts.answer: transported in blood / plasma; dissolved; hepatic vein to vena cava / heart; renal artery (to kidney); to kidney; urea removed / filtered + from blood; part of urine; ureter (to bladder); bladder; (bladder to) urethra

parts.question: Urea is a molecule which contains nitrogen. Suggest why it is important that molecules that contain nitrogen are added to the soil.
parts.answer: cycling / replenishing nitrogen; used / converted by nitrifying bacteria / nitrogen fixing bacteria; form + nitrates; nitrates + taken up by plants; production of proteins / amino acids; increased soil fertility (for crops) / plants grow faster / bigger; animals / herbivores obtain protein / nitrogen from plants

questions.question: Rice, maize and wheat plants are the main carbohydrate source for more than 60% of the human population.

parts.question: Describe how plants like these produce carbohydrates using materials from the environment.
parts.answer: photosynthesis; in leaves / green parts of plants / in mesophyll / chloroplasts; using chlorophyll; using light energy; water + soil / irrigation / roots / root hair cells; carbon dioxide + air / through stomata; produce glucose / C6H12O6; starch; magnesium + from soil + to make chlorophyll

parts.question: Many scientists predict that, as the world's human population increases, there will be a shortage of food. Suggest and explain why an increase in human population is predicted to lead to a global human food shortage.
parts.answer: greater demand for food; not enough land available for farming; land being used for e.g. building / biofuels / deforestation; increase in greenhouse gases; (leads to) climate change; droughts / floods / salinisation; lack of nutrients in soils monoculture / overcrowding

questions.question: Describe how air is breathed in, using the rib cage and diaphragm, so that it reaches the alveoli.

parts.question: Describe how air is breathed in, using the rib cage and diaphragm, so that it reaches the alveoli.
parts.answer: contraction of diaphragm; diaphragm flattens / moves down; bones of rib cage pulled upwards / outwards; external intercostal muscles contract / internal relax; pressure decreases / volume increases; air passes through nose / trachea / windpipe / larynx; through bronchi / bronchioles

parts.question: Bronchitis is a condition that can be triggered by breathing in substances that cause the bronchi to become inflamed and produce a large amount of mucus. Suggest how this condition can affect a person and explain how a person may be able to avoid developing the condition.
parts.answer: (effect on person): bronchi narrowed / blocked by mucus; need to cough + remove; pain (from coughing); increase in bacteria / microorganisms; harder for oxygen to reach alveoli / reduced gaseous exchange; reduced aerobic respiration; ability to exercise reduced; feel tired / lethargic; (avoiding the condition): avoid smoking / avoid breathing in smoke; avoid particulate air pollution / use of mask; avoid places with lots of vehicles
